name: Giant Panda
hint: This bear-like mammal eats almost exclusively one type of plant, consuming up to 12 hours a day. Fewer than 1,900 survive in the wild.

name: Bengal Tiger
hint: The most numerous wild tiger subspecies, known for its striking orange and black stripes. Each individual's pattern is unique, like a fingerprint.

name: Kangaroo
hint: This iconic marsupial can cover 9 metres in a single bound and reach 70 km/h. It raises its young in a pouch for several months after birth.

name: Polar Bear
hint: The world's largest land carnivore depends entirely on sea ice to hunt seals. It can swim over 300 km without rest.

name: Kiwi
hint: This flightless, nocturnal bird lays an egg that can weigh up to 20% of its own body weight — one of the largest egg-to-body ratios of any bird.

name: Mountain Gorilla
hint: The world's largest primate lives in dense rainforest around volcanic mountains. Fewer than 1,100 individuals remain in the wild.

name: Ring-tailed Lemur
hint: All lemur species evolved in total isolation on a single island for over 60 million years — found absolutely nowhere else on Earth.

name: Capybara
hint: The world's largest rodent is highly social and semi-aquatic. It can remain submerged for up to 5 minutes to escape predators.

name: Komodo Dragon
hint: The world's heaviest lizard can take down prey much larger than itself using bacteria-laden saliva and venom. It can weigh over 70 kg.

name: Fennec Fox
hint: The world's smallest fox has ears proportionally larger than any other carnivore — they act as radiators to dissipate heat in extreme temperatures.

name: Jaguar
hint: The largest cat in the Americas kills by crushing the skull of its prey — a unique hunting technique among big cats. It is an excellent swimmer.

name: Axolotl
hint: Unlike most amphibians, this salamander never undergoes metamorphosis — it retains its larval features for life. It can regenerate entire limbs.

name: Platypus
hint: One of only five egg-laying mammals alive today. It hunts using electroreception — sensing the electric fields produced by muscle contractions of prey.

name: Red Panda
hint: This rusty-furred animal is the only living member of its own family. Despite the name, it is not closely related to the giant panda.

name: Snow Leopard
hint: This elusive big cat uses its long, thick tail as a blanket while sleeping in extreme cold. It cannot roar — it can only chuff, hiss and mew.

name: Saiga Antelope
hint: This antelope's oversized, bulbous nose acts as a natural filter in dusty summers and a warming chamber for frozen winter air.

name: Proboscis Monkey
hint: Found only in the coastal mangroves and riverine forests of a single large island. The male's enormous nose is thought to attract mates.

name: Aye-aye
hint: The world's largest nocturnal primate taps on bark with its elongated finger to locate insect larvae by sound — a technique called percussive foraging.

name: Kakapo
hint: The world's heaviest parrot is also the only flightless and nocturnal one. Every individual is known by name — there are fewer than 250 left.

name: Tasmanian Devil
hint: The world's largest carnivorous marsupial has one of the strongest bites relative to body size of any mammal. Its screech can be heard from far away.

name: Ethiopian Wolf
hint: The world's rarest canid lives exclusively above 3,000 metres altitude in Afro-alpine grassland. Fewer than 500 survive.

name: Gharial
hint: This crocodilian's extremely long, narrow snout lined with interlocking teeth is perfectly designed for snatching fish. Males develop a bulbous growth on the tip of their snout.

name: American Bison
hint: Once the most numerous large land animal in the western hemisphere, hunted to fewer than 1,000 individuals before conservation efforts reversed the decline.

name: Moose
hint: The largest member of the deer family. Males shed and regrow their antlers — which can span 1.8 metres — every single year.

name: African Wild Dog
hint: This pack hunter has an 80% hunt success rate — the highest of any large predator. Each individual's coat pattern is completely unique.

name: Vicuña
hint: This wild relative of the llama produces the finest natural animal fibre in the world. It can only be shorn every three years under strict regulations.

name: Siberian Tiger
hint: The world's largest wild cat can weigh over 300 kg and needs to consume around 10 kg of meat per day. Its territory can span over 1,000 km².

name: Andean Condor
hint: With a wingspan up to 3.3 metres, this is one of the world's largest flying birds. It can soar for hours on thermal updrafts without a single wingbeat.

name: Galápagos Tortoise
hint: The world's largest living tortoise can live over 150 years and survive a year without food or water. Darwin's observations of this species helped shape his theory of evolution.

name: African Penguin
hint: The only penguin species native to the African continent. It brays like a donkey — earning it the nickname 'jackass penguin'.
